panels.title: Switch alive (reports in last 2 min)
panels.description: Count of reports received per switch in the last 2 minutes. Green = active, red = no reports (switch down or unplugged).

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int-xd")
  |> range(start: -2m)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and r._field == "seq")
  |> group(columns: ["switch_id"])
  |> count()
  |> keep(columns: ["switch_id", "_value"])

panels.title: Total report rate (seq-based, switch side)
panels.description: True reports/s from the global monotonic seq counter — immune to collector drops. This is what the switch actually generated.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int-xd")
  |> range(start: -1h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and r._field == "seq")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 10s, fn: max, createEmpty: false)
  |> derivative(unit: 1s, nonNegative: true)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value"])

panels.title: Dropped reports (seq gaps)
panels.description: Estimated lost reports per 10s window from gaps in the seq counter. 0 = no loss. Rises when collector can't keep up.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int-xd")
  |> range(start: -1h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and r._field == "seq")
  |> group()
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])
  |> difference(nonNegative: false)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: if r._value > 1.0 then r._value - 1.0 else 0.0 }))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 10s, fn: sum, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value"])

panels.title: Report rate per switch (reports/s)
panels.description: Reports/s per switch_id from count of written records. Use alongside total rate panel to see which switch contributes what. Switch 2 drops to 0 when unplugged.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int-xd")
  |> range(start: -1h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and r._field == "seq")
  |> group(columns: ["switch_id"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 10s, fn: count, createEmpty: false)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: float(v: r._value) / 10.0 }))
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "switch_id"])

panels.title: Report generation time per switch (hop_delay, ns)
panels.description: hop_delay = time the packet spent inside the switch pipeline, stamped by the ASIC. This is how long it takes the switch to process the packet and generate the INT-XD report. Should be hundreds of ns on Tofino hardware.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int-xd")
  |> range(start: -1h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and r._field == "hop_delay")
  |> group(columns: ["switch_id"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 10s, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "switch_id"])

panels.title: Collector congestion: switch rate vs written rate
panels.description: Green = seq-based rate (what the switch actually generated). Yellow = count-based rate (what the collector wrote to InfluxDB). Gap between them = dropped/lost reports. Flat overlap = healthy. Growing gap = collector falling behind.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int-xd")
  |> range(start: -1h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and r._field == "seq")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 10s, fn: max, createEmpty: false)
  |> derivative(unit: 1s, nonNegative: true)
  |> set(key: "_field", value: "switch generated")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "_field"])

from(bucket: "int-xd")
  |> range(start: -1h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and r._field == "seq")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 10s, fn: count, createEmpty: false)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: float(v: r._value) / 10.0 }))
  |> set(key: "_field", value: "collector written")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "_field"])

panels.title: Queue occupancy per switch
panels.description: Max queue depth (cells) per switch per window. Should be ~0 at low rates. Rises under congestion — use this alongside hop latency to confirm queueing is the cause of latency increase.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int-xd")
  |> range(start: -1h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and r._field == "q_occupancy")
  |> group(columns: ["switch_id"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 10s, fn: max, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "switch_id"])

panels.title: Hop latency per switch (egress_tstamp - ingress_tstamp, ns)
panels.description: Per-switch in-pipeline latency computed from real ASIC timestamps. Flat = no congestion. Rises with queue occupancy under load.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int-xd")
  |> range(start: -1h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and (r._field == "ingress_tstamp" or r._field == "egress_tstamp"))
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["_time", "switch_id"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r.egress_tstamp - r.ingress_tstamp }))
  |> group(columns: ["switch_id"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 10s, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "switch_id"])

panels.title: Hop latency percentiles — all switches (p50 / p95 / p99)
panels.description: Latency distribution across all switches. Flat tails under rising load = no congestion. Growing p99 relative to p50 = tail latency / queueing starting.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int-xd")
  |> range(start: -1h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and (r._field == "ingress_tstamp" or r._field == "egress_tstamp"))
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["_time", "switch_id"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r.egress_tstamp - r.ingress_tstamp }))
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 10s, fn: (tables=<-, column) => tables |> quantile(q: 0.50), createEmpty: false)
  |> set(key: "_field", value: "p50")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "_field"])

from(bucket: "int-xd")
  |> range(start: -1h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and (r._field == "ingress_tstamp" or r._field == "egress_tstamp"))
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["_time", "switch_id"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r.egress_tstamp - r.ingress_tstamp }))
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 10s, fn: (tables=<-, column) => tables |> quantile(q: 0.95), createEmpty: false)
  |> set(key: "_field", value: "p95")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "_field"])

from(bucket: "int-xd")
  |> range(start: -1h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and (r._field == "ingress_tstamp" or r._field == "egress_tstamp"))
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["_time", "switch_id"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r.egress_tstamp - r.ingress_tstamp }))
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 10s, fn: (tables=<-, column) => tables |> quantile(q: 0.99), createEmpty: false)
  |> set(key: "_field", value: "p99")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "_field"])

panels.title: Timestamp sanity — raw ingress_tstamp per switch (ns since ASIC boot)
panels.description: Raw ASIC ingress timestamp value. Should be in the trillions of ns (e.g. 2.2e12 = ~37 min uptime). Proves these are real nanosecond hardware clock values, not host-OS timestamps. All 3 switches should track together since it is the same ASIC.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int-xd")
  |> range(start: -1h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and r._field == "ingress_tstamp")
  |> group(columns: ["switch_id"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 10s, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "switch_id"])

panels.title: Inter-arrival time per switch (Δ ingress_tstamp, ns)
panels.description: Time between consecutive packets as seen by each switch ASIC clock. Directly reflects offered packet rate (e.g. 4166 pps ≈ 240 µs = 240,000 ns gap). All 3 switches should read the same gap since it is the same physical flow. stdDev = jitter.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int-xd")
  |> range(start: -1h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and r._field == "ingress_tstamp")
  |> group(columns: ["switch_id"])
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])
  |> difference(nonNegative: true)
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 10s, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "switch_id"])
